title: Streams, Super Streams, and Workload Choice · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for streams, super streams, and workload choice and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 2650 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the quiz Queue Stream Semantics case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the quiz Queue Stream Semantics case

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Workload Selection Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 5124 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Workload Selection Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Workload Selection Evidence through the concrete a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Workload Selection Evidence, keep Workload Selection Evidence connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Workload Selection Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Workload Selection Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Workload Selection Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 5124 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Workload Selection Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch. In Go, amqp091-go queue consumption automatically exposes every stream protocol capability.
